sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jordan Roberts, a 49-year-old male, presented with a history of hypertension and palpitations.
He was recently in the emergency room with high blood pressure and palpitations, which he attributed to a panic attack triggered by consuming coffee and no breakfast.
He has a family history of high blood pressure and has been taking medication for it since he was young.
His current medication regimen includes amlodipine 10 milligrams daily and benicar hct (hydrochlorothiazide) 40-25.
Past medical history: The patient has a long-standing history of hypertension, for which he has been taking medication since he was young.
He has a family history of high blood pressure.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms.
Current medications: Amlodipine 10 milligrams daily; benicar hct (hydrochlorothiazide) 40-25.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's heart and lungs sounded good during the exam.
The patient's ankles and feet showed no signs of swelling.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has resistant hypertension, which is not responding to his current medication regimen.
Problem list: Hypertension; resistant hypertension; unknown.
Plan:
I added spironolactone to the patient's medication regimen to help lower his blood pressure.
I educated the patient on the potential risks of the medication, including electrolyte disturbances, dehydration, and gynecomastia.
I instructed the patient to hydrate regularly and to have regular blood tests to monitor his electrolyte levels.
I also advised the patient to reduce his salt intake and to follow the dash diet.
I scheduled a follow-up appointment in two weeks to check his blood pressure and electrolytes.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again in two weeks to check his blood pressure and electrolytes.
